subject: DevOps
title: Stages, Jobs, and Steps
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Stages, Jobs, and Steps.

hook.text: A pipeline has three layers of organization: stages, jobs, and steps. Each nests inside the next.

buildup.text: A stage is a phase: build, test, deploy. Stages run in order. The test stage waits for build to finish.

discovery.text: A job is a unit of work inside a stage. Multiple jobs in one stage can run in parallel on separate machines.

twist.text: A step is a single command inside a job: run a script, install a package, upload an artifact.

climax.text: Stages for sequence, jobs for parallelism, steps for actions. Understanding this hierarchy is key to fast pipelines.

punchline.text: Stages, jobs, steps. Sequence, parallelism, action.

quiz.question: How can multiple jobs within a single stage speed up a pipeline?
options: They can run in parallel on separate machines
They reduce the number of steps needed
